title: Scaling law of asymptotic freedom in collective charging of quantum batteries
authors: Gentaro Watanabe, Chunlin Chen, B. Prasanna Venkatesh
abstract: We establish a universal scaling law for collective charging of quantum batteries, independent of microscopic details. We prove that the ergotropy-to-energy ratio approaches unity at least as fast as $\sim N^{-1}$ with the number of batteries $N$, implying generic asymptotic freedom. We further show how the universal $1/N$ scaling can be overcome: when the battery state becomes asymptotically pure, the convergence can be substantially faster, including $\sim N^{-b}$ with $b>1$ and even exponential scaling in $N^2$. Rigorous finite-$N$ upper and lower bounds on the ergotropy-to-energy ratio are further derived, providing nonasymptotic guarantees for the universal $1/N$ scaling.
summary: This paper rigorously derives universal scaling laws governing how efficiently stored energy can be extracted from a collection of $N$ coupled quantum batteries. It establishes that while generic charging leads to an asymptotic freedom characterized by $\sim 1/N$ decay, specific conditions allow for significantly faster convergence, including exponential scaling. The findings provide crucial theoretical bounds for designing high-efficiency quantum energy devices.
main_problem: The paper establishes a universal scaling law for the collective charging of quantum batteries (QBs) and investigates the phenomenon of 'asymptotic freedom,' where stored energy becomes nearly fully extractable.
main_result: It proves that the ergotropy-to-energy ratio approaches unity, exhibiting generic asymptotic freedom with at least $\sim N^{-1}$ scaling, but can achieve much faster convergence ($\sim N^{-b}, b>1$ or exponential) if the battery state becomes asymptotically pure.
method: The analysis involves deriving rigorous finite-$N$ upper and lower bounds on the ergotropy-to-energy ratio by analyzing the residual population $\delta(N)$ within the framework of collective charging dynamics.
model_or_system: The system consists of $N$ identical, non-interacting quantum batteries (QBs), modeled as qudits. The dynamics are studied using concepts related to total energy ($E_B$) and ergotropy ($	ext{Erg}$), often involving the GKLS master equation for open charging.
key_observables: Total Energy ($E_B$), Ergotropy ($	ext{Erg}$), Figure of Merit ($	ext{Erg}/E_B$), Residual Population ($\delta(N)$).
important_parameters: Number of batteries ($N$), dimension of the qudits ($d$), and the asymptotic behavior of the residual population ($\delta_\infty$).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes identical, non-interacting QBs and often requires assumptions about the battery state remaining mixed or approaching purity in specific limits.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the concept of asymptotic freedom showing generic $\sim N^{-1}$ scaling, alongside examples demonstrating faster convergence rates such as power-law ($\sim 1/N^b$) and exponential ($\sim e^{-bN^2}$) scaling for different charging regimes.
paper_structure: The paper develops by first establishing the general $1/N$ universal scaling law (Theorem 1) based on residual population bounds. It then analyzes specific cases, distinguishing between scenarios where the asymptotic residual population is zero or non-zero, leading to derivations of faster convergence rates in those special limits.
why_interesting: This work provides fundamental theoretical guarantees regarding energy extraction efficiency in quantum systems, directly impacting the design principles for quantum energy storage devices and understanding collective quantum phenomena.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Emergence of asymptotic freedom in collective quantum battery charging. Left: an ensemble of N identical d-level batteries with arbitrary spectra. Right: representative scaling of the ergotropy- to-energy ratio EB/EB toward unity with increasing N, including the generic ∼N−1 scaling and accelerated convergence.

FIG. 2. Demonstration of 1/N scaling toward asymptotic freedom for 1 −(EB/EB), together with the bounds given by Eqs. (10) and (23), for unitary charging of the d = 3 Dicke model. The inset shows the purity as a function of N. The parameter values are ωC = ωB and g = 20ωB.

FIG. 3. Demonstration of the universal 1/N scaling toward asymp- totic freedom. All models considered collapse onto a single line with slope −1 in the log-log plot. The parameter values are the same as Fig. 2.

FIG. 4. Demonstration of fast convergence to asymptotic freedom with power-law scaling ∼1/Nb with b > 1. The green solid line indicates a fit ∼1/N7.2, while the blue dashed line shows the ∼1/N scaling for reference. The inset displays the purity as a function of N. The parameters are set to Ω= ωB = 5 and γ = 1

FIG. 5. Demonstration of fast convergence to asymptotic free- dom with exponential scaling. The blue solid line indicates a fit ∼exp(−bN2) with b = 0.06. The inset displays the purity as a func- tion of N. The parameters are set to Ω= 0, ωB = 10, and γ = 1.
